overview: Add a file browser and code editor to web-terminal. The sidebar gets a new tab to switch between session list and file tree view. The file tree shows the current session's working directory with lazy-loaded expandable folders. Clicking a file opens a CodeMirror 6 editor replacing the terminal view, with syntax highlighting for common languages and auto-save. Full file management includes create, edit, delete, rename, upload (drag & drop + file picker), and download (single file or folder as zip).
goals: Browse current session's working directory as a file tree in the sidebar
Open and edit text files with CodeMirror 6 and syntax highlighting
Auto-save edited files with visual feedback
Full file management: create, delete, rename files and folders
Upload files via drag-and-drop and file picker, download files and folders as zip
Toggle hidden files visibility, default hidden
Mobile-friendly: full functionality on both desktop and mobile
nonGoals: No multi-file tabs (one file open at a time)
No terminal-in-editor split view (editor replaces terminal, switch back to terminal)
No binary file editing (images, executables shown as download-only)
successMetrics: File tree loads and displays within 1 second for directories with < 200 entries
Editor opens a file with correct syntax highlighting within 500ms
Auto-save triggers within 1 second of last keystroke
Upload and download work for single files and folders (zip)
All features work on mobile viewport (390px width)
openQuestions: Should we show file size and modification time in the file tree?
Should there be a search/filter within the file tree?

newDependencies: multer or busboy (npm, for file upload)

routes.name: Main App
routes.purpose: Single-page app — file browser added to sidebar, editor replaces terminal view

uiNotes: Sidebar has two tabs at top: 'Sessions' and 'Files', switching the sidebar content
File tree: folder icons, file icons, indentation for nesting, lazy-load on folder click
Hidden files toggle button in file tree header
Right-click or long-press context menu for: rename, delete, download, new file, new folder
Editor view: toolbar with filename, save status indicator, close button to return to terminal
Editor toolbar shows language name and line/column position
Upload: drag-and-drop zone on file tree area + upload button in file tree header
Download folder: triggers zip generation on server, then browser download
File size limit: 1MB for editing, larger files shown as download-only
Mobile: sidebar file tree same as desktop, editor full-width replacing terminal
rules: File tree is per-session: shows the cwd of the active session's PTY process
Lazy loading: only load directory contents when user expands a folder
Default hidden: files starting with . are hidden, toggled via button
node_modules, .git, __pycache__ hidden by default (same toggle controls all)
File size limit: files > 1MB open as download-only, not in editor
Auto-save: debounce 1 second after last edit, then PUT to server
Syntax highlighting detection by file extension

Language map: .js/.jsx -> javascript, .ts/.tsx -> typescript, .py -> python, .go -> go, .rs -> rust, .json -> json, .yaml/.yml -> yaml, .md -> markdown, .sh/.bash -> shell, .html -> html, .css -> css, .sql -> sql, .toml -> toml, .xml -> xml
Upload via multipart form data, supports multiple files
Folder download: server creates zip archive on-the-fly via archiver, streams to client
When switching sessions, file tree refreshes to new session's cwd
When editor is open, terminal is hidden; closing editor shows terminal again
qualityGates: Chrome DevTools UI test: sidebar tab switch between Sessions and Files
Chrome DevTools UI test: file tree renders and folder expand works
Chrome DevTools UI test: clicking file opens editor with content
Chrome DevTools UI test: editor close returns to terminal

stories.title: Backend API: get session cwd
stories.description: As a developer, I want an API endpoint to get the current working directory of a session's PTY process so the file tree knows which directory to show.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /api/sessions/:id/cwd endpoint in server.js
Read cwd from the PTY process using /proc/<pid>/cwd readlink or lsof
Return JSON { cwd: '/path/to/dir' }
Example: session running in /home/user/project -> returns { cwd: '/home/user/project' }
Negative case: session not found -> 404 { error: 'Session not found' }

stories.title: Backend API: list directory contents
stories.description: As a developer, I want an API to list directory contents with file metadata so the file tree can render entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /api/fs/list?path=xxx&hidden=true|false endpoint
Return JSON { path: '/abs/path', entries: [{ name, type: 'file'|'directory', size, mtime }] }
Sort: directories first, then files, both alphabetically
When hidden=false (default): exclude files starting with . and directories named node_modules, .git, __pycache__, .next, dist, build
When hidden=true: include all entries
Example: list /home/user/project -> returns sorted entries with types and sizes
Negative case: path does not exist -> 404 { error: 'Directory not found' }
Negative case: path is a file, not directory -> 400 { error: 'Not a directory' }

stories.title: Backend API: read file content
stories.description: As a developer, I want an API to read file contents so the editor can display them.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /api/fs/read?path=xxx endpoint
Return JSON { path, content, size, mtime }
If file size > 1MB, return { path, size, mtime, tooLarge: true } without content field
Detect binary files (check for null bytes in first 8KB), return { path, size, binary: true }
Example: read /home/user/project/index.js -> returns file content as string
Negative case: file not found -> 404
Negative case: path is a directory -> 400

stories.title: Backend API: write file content
stories.description: As a developer, I want an API to save file contents so the editor can auto-save.
acceptanceCriteria: Add PUT /api/fs/write endpoint with JSON body { path, content }
Write content to file, create parent directories if needed (recursive mkdir)
Return JSON { ok: true, size, mtime }
Reject if content length > 1MB -> 413 { error: 'File too large' }
Example: write to /home/user/project/test.js -> file saved, returns size and mtime
Negative case: path is a directory -> 400
Negative case: permission denied -> 403

stories.title: Backend API: create, rename, delete files and folders
stories.description: As a developer, I want APIs for file management operations so the UI can create, rename, and delete entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /api/fs/create with { path, type: 'file'|'directory' } — creates empty file or mkdir
Add POST /api/fs/rename with { oldPath, newPath } — renames file or directory
Add DELETE /api/fs/delete with { path } — deletes file or directory (recursive for dirs)
All operations return { ok: true }
Example: create file /home/user/project/new.js -> empty file created
Example: rename /home/user/project/old.js to new.js -> file renamed
Example: delete /home/user/project/temp/ -> directory removed recursively
Negative case: create at existing path -> 409 { error: 'Already exists' }
Negative case: rename to existing path -> 409
Negative case: delete non-existent path -> 404

stories.title: Backend API: file upload (multipart)
stories.description: As a developer, I want an upload API so users can upload files to the server.
acceptanceCriteria: Install multer: npm install multer, add to package.json dependencies
Add POST /api/fs/upload endpoint accepting multipart/form-data
Form field 'dest' specifies target directory path
Support multiple file uploads in one request
Save uploaded files to the dest directory, preserving original filenames
Return JSON { ok: true, files: [{ name, size }] }
Example: upload 2 files to /home/user/project/ -> both saved, response lists them
Negative case: dest directory does not exist -> create it automatically
Negative case: file with same name exists -> overwrite

stories.title: Backend API: folder download as zip
stories.description: As a developer, I want an API to download a folder as a zip archive.
acceptanceCriteria: Install archiver: npm install archiver, add to package.json dependencies
Add GET /api/fs/download?path=xxx endpoint
If path is a file: stream file directly with Content-Disposition attachment header
If path is a directory: create zip archive on-the-fly using archiver, stream to client
Set appropriate Content-Type and Content-Disposition headers
Example: download /home/user/project/src/ -> streams src.zip containing all files
Example: download /home/user/project/index.js -> streams the file directly
Negative case: path not found -> 404

stories.title: Sidebar tab switching: Sessions vs Files
stories.description: As a user, I want to switch between session list and file tree in the sidebar so I can manage both sessions and files.
acceptanceCriteria: Add two tab buttons at top of sidebar: 'Sessions' and 'Files'
Clicking 'Sessions' shows the existing session list (current behavior)
Clicking 'Files' shows the file tree for the active session's cwd
Active tab has highlighted style, inactive tab is dimmed
Default tab is 'Sessions'
When switching to Files tab, fetch active session's cwd via GET /api/sessions/:id/cwd, then load root directory
When active session changes, if Files tab is active, refresh the file tree
Style tabs to match VS Code sidebar tab style (border-bottom indicator)
Example: click Files tab -> file tree loads for current session's directory
Negative case: no active session -> Files tab shows 'No active session' message

stories.title: File tree component with lazy loading
stories.description: As a user, I want to see a file tree in the sidebar that lazy-loads folder contents so I can navigate the project structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Render file tree in sidebar Files tab area
Show folder icon for directories, file icon for files (use unicode or simple CSS icons)
Indent nested items with consistent spacing (16px per level)
Click folder to expand/collapse, loads contents via /api/fs/list on first expand
Cache expanded folder contents, re-fetch on manual refresh
Show loading indicator while fetching directory contents
Hidden files toggle button in file tree header, persisted in localStorage
File tree header shows current directory path (truncated)
Click file to open in editor (calls editor open function from US-011)
Example: expand src/ folder -> loads and shows src/ contents indented
Negative case: empty directory -> shows '(empty)' placeholder

stories.title: File tree context menu: create, rename, delete
stories.description: As a user, I want right-click context menu on file tree items for file management actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click (desktop) or long-press (mobile) on file/folder shows context menu
Menu items: New File, New Folder, Rename, Delete, Download
New File/Folder: shows inline input field in the tree for name entry
Rename: shows inline input with current name pre-filled
Delete: shows confirmation dialog before executing
Download: triggers /api/fs/download for the selected path
After create/rename/delete, refresh the parent directory in the tree
Context menu positioned near the clicked item, stays within viewport
Click outside context menu closes it
Example: right-click folder -> select New File -> type name -> file created, tree refreshed
Negative case: rename to empty name -> rejected, input stays open

stories.title: CodeMirror 6 editor integration
stories.description: As a user, I want to open files in a code editor with syntax highlighting so I can view and edit code in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Include CodeMirror 6 core and language packages via CDN (codemirror, @codemirror/lang-javascript, @codemirror/lang-python, @codemirror/lang-json, @codemirror/lang-markdown, @codemirror/lang-html, @codemirror/lang-css, @codemirror/lang-yaml, @codemirror/lang-xml, @codemirror/lang-sql)
Add oneDark theme from @codemirror/theme-one-dark for dark mode compatibility
When a file is clicked in the tree, fetch content via /api/fs/read
Hide terminal container, show editor container in its place
Detect language from file extension and apply correct syntax highlighting
Editor toolbar: filename display, language label, line:col position, save status, close button
Close button hides editor, shows terminal again, refocuses terminal
If file is > 1MB (tooLarge) or binary: show message with download button instead of editor
Example: click index.js -> editor opens with JavaScript highlighting
Example: click data.json -> editor opens with JSON highlighting
Negative case: click binary file -> shows 'Binary file, download only' with download button

stories.title: Editor auto-save with status indicator
stories.description: As a user, I want my edits to auto-save so I don't lose changes.
acceptanceCriteria: On editor content change, start a 1-second debounce timer
After debounce, PUT /api/fs/write with current content
Status indicator in editor toolbar: 'Unsaved' (yellow dot), 'Saving...' (spinner), 'Saved' (green check), 'Error' (red x with message)
If save fails, show error message and keep 'Unsaved' state
Track dirty state: warn user if closing editor with unsaved changes
Keyboard shortcut Ctrl+S / Cmd+S triggers immediate save (bypass debounce)
Example: type in editor -> 'Unsaved' appears -> 1s later -> 'Saving...' -> 'Saved'
Negative case: server returns error -> status shows 'Save failed: [message]', content preserved

stories.title: File upload: drag-and-drop and file picker
stories.description: As a user, I want to upload files via drag-and-drop or file picker so I can add files to the project.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload button in file tree header opens native file picker (multiple files allowed)
Drag files over the file tree area -> visual drop zone indicator appears
Drop files -> upload to currently expanded/selected directory via /api/fs/upload
Show upload progress or spinner during upload
After upload complete, refresh the target directory in file tree
Support uploading folders (if browser supports webkitdirectory)
Example: drag 3 files onto src/ folder -> files uploaded to src/, tree refreshed
Negative case: upload fails -> show error toast, tree not refreshed

stories.title: Folder download as zip
stories.description: As a user, I want to download a folder as a zip file so I can export project files.
acceptanceCriteria: Download option in context menu triggers zip download for folders, direct download for files
Browser starts download with appropriate filename (folder-name.zip or filename)
Show brief loading indicator in context menu or toast while zip is being generated
Example: right-click src/ -> Download -> browser downloads src.zip
Example: right-click index.js -> Download -> browser downloads index.js
Negative case: very large directory -> server streams zip, no timeout for reasonable sizes (< 100MB)

stories.title: Editor and file tree styles matching theme system
stories.description: As a user, I want the file tree and editor to match my selected color theme.
acceptanceCriteria: File tree uses CSS variables (--bg-sidebar, --text, --text-dim, --border, --bg-hover, --accent)
Editor uses CodeMirror oneDark theme adapted to match current CSS variables
Sidebar tabs styled consistently with existing sidebar header
Context menu styled with theme colors
Upload drop zone styled with theme accent color
All new UI elements adapt when theme is switched
Mobile responsive: file tree fills sidebar width, editor fills full viewport
Example: switch from VS Code Dark to Dracula -> file tree and editor colors update
Negative case: default theme (VS Code Dark) -> all elements render correctly

stories.title: End-to-end and UI testing
stories.description: As a developer, I want automated tests to verify the file browser and editor work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Server starts without errors with node server.js (including new dependencies)
Chrome DevTools test: sidebar shows Sessions and Files tabs
Chrome DevTools test: clicking Files tab loads file tree
Chrome DevTools test: expanding a folder shows child entries
Chrome DevTools test: clicking a .js file opens editor with content
Chrome DevTools test: close button returns to terminal view
Chrome DevTools test: hidden files toggle works
API test: GET /api/fs/list returns entries for valid directory
API test: GET /api/fs/read returns content for valid file
API test: PUT /api/fs/write saves content and returns ok
Example: full flow — Files tab -> expand folder -> click file -> edit -> auto-save -> close -> back to terminal
Negative case: API returns 404 for non-existent path
